story: On the second night, Ben accepted Nate’s offer to sleep in the cedar-scented trailer. In the dark, they kissed, and in the morning they held each other. Just before dawn, Emily had walked to the cooler and, passing the trailer’s small window, saw two silhouettes leaning close and heard a soft laugh; she turned away and kept it to herself. During pack-up, Nate told Ben, “Don’t pretend it didn’t happen.” Ben hovered between the familiar and this new path.
implausible_knowledge: Just before dawn, Emily had walked to the cooler and, passing the trailer’s small window, saw two silhouettes leaning close and heard a soft laugh; she turned away and kept it to herself.
question: After the trip, when his friends casually ask him how the weekend was, what will Ben most likely say?
answer_with_knowledge: Acknowledge that he and Nate got close during the trip instead of brushing it off.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep it vague, shrugging it off with “Just camping,” and avoid mentioning what happened with Nate.
